req.title: Hr-Admin updates onboarding-task
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update a onboarding-task through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
Here are comprehensive pytest tests for the onboarding task update requirement:

```python
"""
Tests for HR Admin - Update Onboarding Task
Requirement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update an onboarding-task
through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record
must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from uuid import uuid4


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def hr_admin_user():
    """Authenticated HR-admin user."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid4()),
        "username": "hr.admin@example.com",
        "role": "hr-admin",
        "is_authenticated": True,
        "is_active": True,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def non_hr_admin_user():
    """Authenticated user without HR-admin role."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid4()),
        "username": "employee@example.com",
        "role": "employee",
        "is_authenticated": True,
        "is_active": True,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Unauthenticated (anonymous) user."""
    return {
        "id": None,
        "username": None,
        "role": None,
        "is_authenticated": False,
        "is_active": False,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def existing_onboarding_task():
    """A persisted onboarding task that can be updated."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid4()),
        "title": "Complete tax forms",
        "description": "Fill in W-4 and direct-deposit paperwork",
        "due_days_after_start": 3,
        "assigned_role": "new-hire",
        "is_mandatory": True,
        "status": "active",
        "created_at": datetime(2024, 1, 10, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
        "updated_at": datetime(2024, 1, 10, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
    }


@pytest.fixture
def valid_update_payload():
    """Valid form payload for updating an onboarding task."""
    return {
        "title": "Complete tax forms (updated)",
        "description": "Fill in W-4, I-9 and direct-deposit paperwork",
        "due_days_after_start": 5,
        "assigned_role": "new-hire",
        "is_mandatory": True,
        "status": "active",
    }


@pytest.fixture
def mock_task_repository():
    repo = MagicMock()
    return repo


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_service():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def mock_permission_service():
    service = MagicMock()
    service.has_role.return_value = True
    return service


@pytest.fixture
def onboarding_task_service(
    mock_task_repository, mock_audit_service, mock_permission_service
):
    """
    Thin wrapper that wires the service under test with mocked collaborators.
    Replace with your actual import, e.g.:
        from app.services.onboarding import OnboardingTaskService
    """
    from app.services.onboarding import OnboardingTaskService  # adjust path

    return OnboardingTaskService(
        repository=mock_task_repository,
        audit_service=mock_audit_service,
        permission_service=mock_permission_service,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _task_not_found_side_effect(task_id):
    raise LookupError(f"Onboarding task {task_id!r} not found")


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Authentication & Authorization
# ===========================================================================

class TestAccessControl:
    """Only authenticated HR-admins may update onboarding tasks."""

    def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        unauthenticated_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_permission_service,
    ):
        mock_permission_service.has_role.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError, match="Authentication required"):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=unauthenticated_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=valid_update_payload,
            )

    def test_non_hr_admin_cannot_update(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        non_hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_permission_service,
    ):
        mock_permission_service.has_role.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError, match="Insufficient permissions"):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=non_hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=valid_update_payload,
            )

    def test_hr_admin_is_permitted_to_update(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        mock_permission_service,
    ):
        mock_permission_service.has_role.return_value = True
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = {
            **existing_onboarding_task,
            **valid_update_payload,
        }

        result = onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        assert result is not None

    def test_permission_check_uses_hr_admin_role(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        mock_permission_service,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = {
            **existing_onboarding_task,
            **valid_update_payload,
        }

        onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        mock_permission_service.has_role.assert_called_once_with(
            hr_admin_user, "hr-admin"
        )


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Mandatory Field Validation
# ===========================================================================

class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:
    """All mandatory form fields must be present and non-empty."""

    MANDATORY_FIELDS = ["title", "assigned_role", "due_days_after_start"]

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_raises_validation_error(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        payload = {k: v for k, v in valid_update_payload.items() if k != missing_field}

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=missing_field):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=payload,
            )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_does_not_persist(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        payload = {k: v for k, v in valid_update_payload.items() if k != missing_field}

        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=payload,
            )

        mock_task_repository.update.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_does_not_create_audit_record(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        payload = {k: v for k, v in valid_update_payload.items() if k != missing_field}

        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=payload,
            )

        mock_audit_service.log.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("blank_value", ["", "   ", None])
    def test_blank_title_raises_validation_error(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        blank_value,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "title": blank_value}

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="title"):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=payload,
            )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("blank_value", ["", "   ", None])
    def test_blank_assigned_role_raises_validation_error(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        blank_value,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "assigned_role": blank_value}

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="assigned_role"):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=payload,
            )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("invalid_value", [0, -1, -100])
    def test_non_positive_due_days_raises_validation_error(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        invalid_value,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "due_days_after_start": invalid_value}

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="due_days_after_start"):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=payload,
            )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("invalid_value", ["five", None, 3.5])
    def test_non_integer_due_days_raises_validation_error(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        invalid_value,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "due_days_after_start": invalid_value}

        with pytest.raises((ValueError, TypeError)):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=payload,
            )

    def test_title_exceeding_max_length_raises_validation_error(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "title": "A" * 256}  # exceeds 255 char limit

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="title"):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=payload,
            )

    def test_invalid_status_value_raises_validation_error(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "status": "unknown-status"}

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="status"):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=payload,
            )

    def test_multiple_validation_errors_are_all_reported(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        mock_task_repository,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        payload = {
            "title": "",
            "assigned_role": None,
            "due_days_after_start": -1,
            "status": "active",
        }

        with pytest.raises(ValueError) as exc_info:
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=payload,
            )

        error_message = str(exc_info.value)
        assert "title" in error_message
        assert "assigned_role" in error_message
        assert "due_days_after_start" in error_message


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Successful Update
# ===========================================================================

class TestSuccessfulUpdate:
    """Happy-path scenarios for updating an onboarding task."""

    def test_update_returns_updated_task(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
    ):
        updated_task = {**existing_onboarding_task, **valid_update_payload}
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = updated_task

        result = onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        assert result["title"] == valid_update_payload["title"]
        assert result["description"] == valid_update_payload["description"]
        assert result["due_days_after_start"] == valid_update_payload["due_days_after_start"]

    def test_update_calls_repository_with_correct_data(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = {
            **existing_onboarding_task,
            **valid_update_payload,
        }

        onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        mock_task_repository.update.assert_called_once_with(
            existing_onboarding_task["id"], valid_update_payload
        )

    def test_update_task_id_is_unchanged(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
    ):
        updated_task = {**existing_onboarding_task, **valid_update_payload}
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = updated_task

        result = onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        assert result["id"] == existing_onboarding_task["id"]

    def test_update_with_optional_description_omitted(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
    ):
        payload_without_description = {
            k: v for k, v in valid_update_payload.items() if k != "description"
        }
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = {
            **existing_onboarding_task,
            **payload_without_description,
        }

        result = onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=payload_without_description,
        )

        assert result is not None
        mock_task_repository.update.assert_called_once()

    def test_update_trims_whitespace_from_title(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
    ):
        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "title": "  Complete tax forms (updated)  "}
        expected_title = "Complete tax forms (updated)"
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = {
            **existing_onboarding_task,
            "title": expected_title,
        }

        onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=payload,
        )

        actual_call_data = mock_task_repository.update.call_args[0][1]
        assert actual_call_data["title"] == expected_title


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Audit Record
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditRecord:
    """An audit record must be written exactly once on every successful update."""

    def test_audit_record_written_on_success(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = {
            **existing_onboarding_task,
            **valid_update_payload,
        }

        onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        mock_audit_service.log.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_actor_id(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = {
            **existing_onboarding_task,
            **valid_update_payload,
        }

        onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        audit_call_kwargs = mock_audit_service.log.call_args[1]
        assert audit_call_kwargs.get("actor_id") == hr_admin_user["id"]

    def test_audit_record_contains_task_id(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = {
            **existing_onboarding_task,
            **valid_update_payload,
        }

        onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        audit_call_kwargs = mock_audit_service.log.call_args[1]
        assert audit_call_kwargs.get("resource_id") == existing_onboarding_task["id"]

    def test_audit_record_action_is_update(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = {
            **existing_onboarding_task,
            **valid_update_payload,
        }

        onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        audit_call_kwargs = mock_audit_service.log.call_args[1]
        assert audit_call_kwargs.get("action") == "UPDATE_ONBOARDING_TASK"

    def test_audit_record_contains_timestamp(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = {
            **existing_onboarding_task,
            **valid_update_payload,
        }

        before = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        after = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        audit_call_kwargs = mock_audit_service.log.call_args[1]
        timestamp = audit_call_kwargs.get("timestamp")
        assert timestamp is not None
        assert before <= timestamp <= after

    def test_audit_record_contains_changed_fields(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = {
            **existing_onboarding_task,
            **valid_update_payload,
        }

        onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        audit_call_kwargs = mock_audit_service.log.call_args[1]
        changes = audit_call_kwargs.get("changes")
        assert changes is not None
        assert "title" in changes
        assert changes["title"]["before"] == existing_onboarding_task["title"]
        assert changes["title"]["after"] == valid_update_payload["title"]

    def test_audit_record_not_written_on_validation_failure(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        invalid_payload = {**valid_update_payload, "title": ""}

        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=invalid_payload,
            )

        mock_audit_service.log.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_record_not_written_on_permission_failure(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        non_hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_audit_service,
        mock_permission_service,
    ):
        mock_permission_service.has_role.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=non_hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=valid_update_payload,
            )

        mock_audit_service.log.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_record_not_written_when_repository_raises(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        existing_onboarding_task,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_onboarding_task
        mock_task_repository.update.side_effect = RuntimeError("DB unavailable")

        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=hr_admin_user,
                task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
                data=valid_update_payload,
            )

        mock_audit_service.log.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 5. Task-not-found & Edge Cases
# ===========================================================================

class TestEdgeCases:
    """Edge cases and boundary conditions."""

    def test_update_nonexistent_task_raises_lookup_error(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.side_effect = _task_not_found_side_effect

        with pytest.raises(LookupError):
            onboarding_task_service.update(
                actor=hr_admin_user,
                task_id=str(uuid4()),
                data=valid_update_payload,
            )

    def test_update_nonexistent_task_does_not_write_audit(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_update_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        mock_task_repository.get_by_id.side_effect = _task_not_found_side_effect

            "status": existing_onboarding_task["status"],
        }

        mock_task_repository.update.return_value = existing_onboarding_task

        onboarding_task_service.update(
            actor=hr_admin_user,
            task_id=existing_onboarding_task["id"],
            data=unchanged_payload,
        )

        mock_audit_service.log.assert_called_once()
